article_title: Scala 3.8.4 Ships Security Hardening From the OSTIF and Quarkslab Audit, Patching a Scaladoc XSS and a TASTy Parser Infinite Loop
summary: All four sources read and verified; every quote verbatim and every specific traced. The only automated finding is an off-allowlist-domain warning for two reputable primary sources (scala-lang.org, blog.quarkslab.com), which is a provenance/allowlist-coverage note, not a reader-facing factual error — no corrections record is warranted.

content_preview.title: Scala 3.8.4 Ships Security Hardening From the OSTIF and Quarkslab Audit, Patching a Scaladoc XSS and a TASTy Parser Infinite Loop
content_preview.summary: The maintenance release folds in fixes from Scala's first external security audit, which found no critical or major issues but flagged a stored XSS in Scaladoc and an infinite loop in TASTy parsing.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Scala team released Scala 3.8.4 on June 5, 2026, a maintenance build whose headline content is a set of fixes drawn from an external security audit of the language's codebase. According to the [Scala Programming Language](https://www.scala-lang.org/news/3.8.4/) release notes, the version "includes improvements and fixes for issues discovered during the Scala codebase security audit carried out in collaboration with the Open Source Technology Improvement Fund and Quarkslab."

The...

recommendations: Consider adding scala-lang.org and blog.quarkslab.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — both are reputable primary sources for Scala release and security-audit coverage.

editor_notes.content_quality: Tightly written 672-word News piece with a clean Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis structure. Technical depth (PR numbers, the specific TASTy/Scaladoc/process-parser/deserialization findings) is accurate and appropriately scoped. No editorializing.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four source snapshots read from disk (decompressed) and verified against the article. source-0.html.gz (scala-lang.org/news/3.8.4, HTTP 200) confirms the 'Friday 5 June 2026' release date, the verbatim audit-collaboration quote, all four fix entries with PR numbers (#25676, #25681, #25675, #25678), and the ':help' usability quote. source-1.html.gz (Scala Center blog, HTTP 200) confirms the 'Monday 1 June 2026' date, authors Darja Jovanovic and Solal Pirelli, 'No critical or major security issues were identified during the audit', the 'Sovereign Tech Fund investment' phrasing, the assessment/supply-chain split, and the '5 medium / 1 low / 2 informational' tally attributed to the Scala Center. source-2.html.gz (Quarkslab, HTTP 200) confirms 'Posted Mon 01 June 2026', authors Sébastien Rolland and Samuel Hangouet, the verbatim '9 vulnerabilities ... 5 of medium severity, 2 of low severity, and 2 informative issues' tally, the scope quote, the Gadget Inspector/Opengrep and fuzzing quotes, and the MEDIUM-1 deserialization-gadget, MEDIUM-6 Scaladoc XSS, and MEDIUM-9 TastyUnpickler infinite-loop titles verbatim. source-3.html.gz (scala/scala3 GitHub release tag 3.8.4, HTTP 200) confirms all four PR titles and the 'Allow `:help` syntax for all settings' entry verbatim. OSTIF is referenced in the body but is correctly NOT cited as a source (its page returned 403 and was not snapshotted).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every direct quote appears verbatim in its cited snapshot and every specific (version 3.8.4, June 5 2026 release, June 1 2026 audit publication, the OSTIF+Quarkslab collaboration, the no-critical/major finding, the Scaladoc stored XSS, the TASTy parser infinite loop, the ProcessBuilderImpl deserialization gadget, the four PR numbers, the author names) traces to a cited source. No hallucinations detected. The article deliberately surfaces the severity-tally discrepancy between the Scala Center ('5 medium, 1 low, 2 informational') and Quarkslab ('5 medium, 2 low, 2 informative'), attributing each count to the outlet that carries it with no silent merge — this is correct and transparent reporting.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism; the headline and summary accurately reflect the sourced facts. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Original topic. No prior Machine Herald article covers Scala 3.8.4 or the Scala security audit. Sits alongside, but does not overlap with, recent programming-language release coverage (Rust 1.96, Elixir 1.20, Gleam, Deno, SvelteKit).
recommendations: Add scala-lang.org and blog.quarkslab.com to config/source_allowlist.txt so future Scala coverage does not trip the off-allowlist warning.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully sourced News submission. The sole automated finding is an off-allowlist-domain warning for two reputable primary sources, which is an internal allowlist-coverage gap rather than a reader-facing factual error; it does not warrant a corrections record. Overriding the script's mechanically-derived APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS to APPROVE. Ready for publication as-is.
